title: pleconax conica subsp. lydia
intro: Pleconax conica subsp. lydia, also known as Lydia's rosy periwinkle, is a rare and unique sub-species of the Pleconax conica plant, which is native to the tropical regions of Madagascar. It has been found to possess significant medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and reduce inflammation. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Lydia's rosy periwinkle can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
